generatedBy: LLM-assisted (see prompts.md for exact prompts used)

testCases.title: All dashboard widgets load successfully on initial page load
preconditions: User is logged in with a valid session
User has at least one widget configured on their dashboard
steps: Log in and navigate to the Dashboard page
Observe all configured widgets as the page loads
testCases.expectedResult: Every widget renders without error within an acceptable time threshold (e.g., <3s); no widget is stuck in a perpetual loading state.

testCases.title: Widget displays a loading skeleton/spinner while data is being fetched
preconditions: Dashboard is being loaded
Network throttling is simulated (slow 3G or delayed API response)
steps: Throttle network speed or delay the widget data API response
Load the Dashboard page
Observe widget state before data arrives
testCases.expectedResult: A loading skeleton, spinner, or placeholder is shown for each widget until its data resolves; layout does not shift abruptly once loaded (no content jump).

testCases.title: Widget shows a graceful error state when its data API call fails
preconditions: Dashboard is loading
Backend API for a specific widget returns 500/timeout
steps: Simulate a failing API response for one widget's data source
Load the Dashboard page
Observe the affected widget
testCases.expectedResult: The failed widget displays a clear error message and optional retry action; other widgets on the dashboard continue to load and function normally (isolated failure).

testCases.title: Widgets load correctly when a user has zero configured widgets
preconditions: User account has no widgets added to the dashboard
steps: Log in as a user with an empty dashboard configuration
Navigate to the Dashboard page
testCases.expectedResult: An empty-state message is displayed (e.g., 'No widgets added yet') with a call-to-action to add widgets, instead of a blank or broken page.

testCases.title: Widget load time remains within SLA under a large number of widgets
preconditions: User has the maximum supported number of widgets configured (e.g., 20+)
steps: Configure the dashboard with the maximum allowed number of widgets
Load the Dashboard page and measure total load time
testCases.expectedResult: All widgets load within the defined performance SLA; page remains responsive (no UI freeze) during concurrent widget data fetches.

testCases.title: Widget displays data that matches the underlying source of truth
preconditions: Known test data exists in the backend/database
steps: Seed or identify known data in the backend for a given widget (e.g., number of open test runs)
Load the Dashboard and locate the corresponding widget
Compare the widget's displayed value against the backend/API value directly
testCases.expectedResult: The value shown in the widget exactly matches the value returned by the underlying API/database, with correct formatting (numbers, dates, currency, etc.).

testCases.title: Widget data updates after underlying data changes (refresh or real-time)
preconditions: Dashboard is loaded and displaying current data

testData.action: create/update/delete a record affecting widget data

steps: Note the current value shown in a widget (e.g., 'Total Test Cases: 50')
Perform an action elsewhere in the app that changes the underlying data (e.g., add a new test case)
Refresh the dashboard or wait for auto-refresh/real-time update
testCases.expectedResult: The widget reflects the updated value accurately after refresh (or automatically, if real-time updates are supported).

testCases.title: Widget aggregation/calculations (sums, averages, percentages) are mathematically correct
preconditions: Widget performs an aggregate calculation (e.g., pass rate %)
steps: Seed data with a known set of pass/fail counts
Load the widget showing the aggregated metric (e.g., Pass Rate)
Manually calculate the expected result and compare
testCases.expectedResult: Displayed aggregate value is mathematically accurate and correctly rounded/formatted.

testCases.title: Widget correctly handles and displays zero or null data without errors
preconditions: Underlying data source returns zero records or null values for the widget
steps: Ensure the widget's data source has no records (e.g., a new project with zero test runs)
Load the Dashboard and observe the widget
testCases.expectedResult: Widget displays a sensible default (e.g., '0' or 'No data available') rather than 'NaN', 'undefined', or a broken chart.

testCases.title: Widget data respects the selected date range/time period filter
preconditions: Widget supports a date range selector
Known data exists across multiple time periods
steps: Select a specific date range on a time-bound widget
Verify displayed data only reflects records within that range by cross-checking against the API/database
testCases.expectedResult: Widget data is scoped exactly to the selected date range; no data from outside the range is included.

testCases.title: Applying a single filter correctly narrows displayed data
preconditions: Dashboard/widget contains a filterable data set with mixed values
steps: Open the filter panel on a data widget/table
Apply a filter for a specific value (e.g., Status = Failed)
Observe the resulting data set
testCases.expectedResult: Only records matching the filter criteria are displayed; record count/summary updates accordingly.

testCases.title: Applying multiple filters combines them using correct AND/OR logic
preconditions: Widget supports multiple simultaneous filters
steps: Apply a first filter (e.g., Status = Failed)
Apply a second filter (e.g., Module = Login)
Observe the resulting data set
testCases.expectedResult: Displayed data matches all applied filter conditions simultaneously (per the app's defined AND/OR logic); results are a correct subset of the unfiltered data.

testCases.title: Clearing filters restores the full unfiltered data set
preconditions: One or more filters are currently applied
steps: Apply one or more filters to a widget
Click 'Clear Filters' or reset action
testCases.expectedResult: All filters are removed and the widget reverts to showing the complete, unfiltered data set.

testCases.title: Sorting a column in ascending and descending order returns correctly ordered results
preconditions: Widget/table supports column sorting
steps: Click the sortable column header once (ascending)
Verify the data order
Click again (descending)
Verify the data order reverses correctly
testCases.expectedResult: Data is correctly sorted in ascending order first, then descending order, with proper handling of data type (numeric, alphabetic, date) during comparison.

testCases.title: Filter and sort settings persist correctly when combined together
preconditions: Widget supports both filtering and sorting simultaneously
steps: Apply a filter to the widget
Apply a sort on a column while the filter is still active
Verify results
testCases.expectedResult: The filtered subset of data is displayed in the correctly sorted order; filter and sort do not override or reset one another.

testCases.title: Filtering with a value that matches no records shows an empty state
preconditions: A filter value exists that matches zero records
steps: Apply a filter with a value known to match no records
Observe the widget
testCases.expectedResult: Widget displays a clear 'No results found' / empty state rather than an error or a stale previous result set.

testCases.title: Dashboard layout adapts correctly on desktop, tablet, and mobile viewports
preconditions: Dashboard is loaded with multiple widgets
steps: Load the Dashboard at a desktop resolution (e.g., 1920x1080)
Resize/re-render at tablet resolution (e.g., 768x1024)
Resize/re-render at mobile resolution (e.g., 375x667)
Observe widget layout at each breakpoint
testCases.expectedResult: Widgets reflow into an appropriate grid/stack per breakpoint; no horizontal scrolling, overlapping elements, or cut-off content at any size.

testCases.title: Widgets remain fully readable and usable on small mobile screens
preconditions: Dashboard is loaded on a mobile-sized viewport
steps: Load the Dashboard on a small mobile viewport
Interact with widget controls (filters, dropdowns, charts)
testCases.expectedResult: Text remains legible without zooming; tappable elements meet minimum touch-target size; charts/tables adapt (e.g., convert to scrollable or simplified view) rather than becoming unusable.

testCases.title: Dashboard supports drag-and-drop widget rearrangement on desktop and gracefully disables/adapts it on touch devices
preconditions: Dashboard supports customizable widget positions
steps: On desktop, drag a widget to a new position and verify it persists
On a touch/mobile viewport, attempt the equivalent rearrangement action
testCases.expectedResult: Desktop drag-and-drop works smoothly and the new layout persists on reload; mobile provides an appropriate alternative (e.g., long-press or a reorder menu) rather than a broken drag interaction.

testCases.title: Dashboard layout handles browser zoom levels without breaking
preconditions: Dashboard is loaded at default zoom
steps: Load the Dashboard
Change browser zoom to 80%, then 150%
Observe widget layout and text at each zoom level
testCases.expectedResult: Layout remains functional and readable at various zoom levels; no overlapping or clipped content.

testCases.title: Admin user sees all dashboard widgets and administrative controls
preconditions: User is logged in with an Admin role
steps: Log in as a user with Admin role
Navigate to the Dashboard
testCases.expectedResult: All widgets, including admin-only widgets (e.g., user management, system health, billing), are visible and accessible.

testCases.title: Standard/limited-role user does not see restricted widgets
preconditions: User is logged in with a limited role (e.g., Viewer or Tester)
steps: Log in as a user with a restricted/limited role
Navigate to the Dashboard
Inspect the list of visible widgets
testCases.expectedResult: Widgets requiring elevated permissions are completely hidden (not merely disabled/grayed out) from the UI for this role.

testCases.title: Restricted widget data is not accessible via direct API call for unauthorized roles
preconditions: User has a role without access to a specific widget's data endpoint
steps: Authenticate as a limited-role user
Directly call the API endpoint backing an admin-only widget, bypassing the UI
testCases.expectedResult: API returns 403 Forbidden (not just a UI-level hide); no restricted data is leaked in the response body.

testCases.title: Dashboard updates widget visibility immediately after a user's role/permissions change
preconditions: User is logged in with a role that lacks access to certain widgets
Admin changes the user's role/permissions during the session
steps: Log in as a limited-role user and confirm restricted widgets are hidden
As an admin (separately), upgrade the user's role/permissions
Refresh the dashboard (or observe real-time update, if supported) for the affected user
testCases.expectedResult: Previously hidden widgets become visible after the permission change is applied and the session/token reflects updated access (no stale cached permissions).

testCases.title: Project-level or team-level scoping restricts widget data to authorized projects only
preconditions: User has access to Project A but not Project B
Both projects have data
steps: Log in as a user scoped only to Project A
Load the Dashboard
Verify widget data only reflects Project A
Attempt to switch context or query data for Project B directly
testCases.expectedResult: Widgets display data only for authorized project(s); any attempt to access Project B's data (via UI or API) is blocked.
